glossary.definition: A subword tokenization algorithm that iteratively merges the most frequent pair of adjacent bytes or characters in a text corpus to build a vocabulary.
An attention mechanism relating different positions of a single sequence in order to compute a representation of the sequence, using query, key, and value projections.
A metric that measures the cosine of the angle between two multi-dimensional vectors, evaluating directional alignment regardless of magnitude.

A non-linear ranking function used by search engines to estimate the relevance of documents to a given search query, improving on TF-IDF by incorporating term frequency saturation and document length normalization.

references.title: Introduction to Information Retrieval

research_papers.authors: Tomas Mikolov, Kai Chen, Greg Corrado, Jeffrey Dean
